CONTENT.Club career: Väisänen made his first team debut during season 2013, in a match against KuPS. He was known as a crucial part of the Honka reserve team Pallohonka.In summer 2014 Väisänen joined AIK. He spent 4 seasons in the club. During season 2015 he was loaned to HIFK.Väisänen made his Seria A debut on 20 August 2017 in a match against Lazio. He was loaned to Crotone for season 2018–19.On 24 August 2019, Väisänen signed to Serie B club Chievo a deal. He made his debut in Chievo in a 1–1 home tie with Empoli on 30 August 2019.On 16 August 2021, he joined Cosenza. He made his debut on 22 August 2021 playing full 90 minutes in a match against Ascoli.On 20 June 2023, Väisänen joined Danish Superliga club OB, signing a deal until June 2026.On 18 January 2024, Väisänen returned to Italy when he was loaned out to Serie B side Ascoli.
CONTENT.Personal life: Väisänen's mother Anna-Liisa Tilus-Väisänen is a prominent television presenter for Yle TV1 and Miss World 1984 semi-finalist. His younger brother Leo Väisänen is also a footballer.

CONTENT.Career statistics: As of match played 20 May 2024.As of match played 20 November 2022Notes
CONTENT.International career: Väisänen made his debut in international football on 5 March 2014 at the age 19 when he was called to represent Finland U21 in a match against San Marino U21. During years 2014-2016 Väisänen was a part of the Finnish national youth squad, which he also captained on occasion.Väisänen made his FIFA World Cup qualification match debut on 6 October 2016 on Laugardalsvöllur when Hans Backe chose him to the starting eleven for a match against Iceland.Väisänen was called up for the UEFA Euro 2020 pre-tournament friendly match against Sweden on 29 May 2021. He was nominated to the Finland national team for the UEFA Euro 2020 tournament but had to withdraw due to an injury and was replaced by Niko Hämäläinen.

SUMMARY:
Sauli Väisänen (born 5 June 1994) is a Finnish professional footballer who plays as a centre-back for OB. He began his senior club career playing for Honka , before signing with AIK at age 20 in 2014.
Väisänen made his international debut for Finland in October 2016, at the age of 22.  He appeared in 5 out of 10 of Finland's UEFA Euro 2020 qualification matches and helped Finland national team for the first time reach European Football Championship tournament's group stage.
